title: Hymenostephium goebelii

The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Hymenostephium goebelii include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Hymenostephium goebelii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
taxonomy: Hymenostephium goebelii is classified within the family Compositae, commonly known as the aster or sunflower family, and is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Carduoideae. This classification places Hymenostephium goebelii within the plant order Asterales. The species Hymenostephium goebelii is a part of the genus Hymenostephium, which comprises several species of flowering plants. These plants are native to Mexico and Central America.
